loc.EN:
The .338 Remington Ultra Magnum is a .338 caliber rifle cartridge introduced by Remington Arms in 2002. It is a beltless, rebated rim cartridge based on the .300 Remington Ultra Magnum case shortened .090" and necked-up to accept a 0.338-inch (.338 caliber) bullet. The .338 Remington Ultra Magnum has a similar case capacity as the .338 Lapua Magnum and somewhat lower than that of the .338-378 Weatherby Magnum. It is one of the most powerful .338-caliber rounds in production. Because this cartridge already operates at very high pressures (65,000 PSI), handloaders cannot realize significant velocity improvements over factory ammunition as many handloaders have done over the years with more conventional, lower pressure rounds. However, they can still tune their own loads for best precision in their specific rifles, as with any other cartridge.
This ammunition-related article is a stub. You can help Wikipedia by expanding it.
(https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/.338_Remington_Ultra_Magnum)
loc.ES:
El .338 Remington Ultra Magnum es un cartucho de rifle introducido calibre .338 introducido en el año 2002 por Remington Arms. El diseño se basa en el casquillo del.300 Remington Ultra Magnum​ reducido en .090" y cuyo cuello se ha holgado para alojar una bala de 0.338 pulgadas. El .338 Remington Ultra Magnum tiene la misma capacidad de carga que el .338 Lapua Magnum​ pero un poco menor que el .338-378 Weatherby Magnum. El .338 opera con presiones muy altas (65,000 PSI), por lo que no es factible recargar para superar la performance de la munición comercial, pero es posible mejorar las cargas para efectos de precisión en rifles específicos.
(https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/.338_Remington_Ultra_Magnum)
